data.title: Crypto Faces Uncertain Future As Trump’s ‘Short-Term Pain’ Plan Unfolds
data.description:
US President Trump’s outspoken acceptance of near-term economic hardship has placed risk assets—including Bitcoin (BTC) and the broader crypto market—under mounting pressure. According to a thread by The Kobeissi Letter on X, President Trump’s strategy revolves around tolerating significant “short term pain” in order to drive down inflation and facilitate the refinancing of over $9 trillion in...
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/crypto-faces-uncertain-future-as-trumps-short-term-pain-plan-unfolds/

data.description:

data.title: CZ proposed 4 suggestions for optimizing the X comment algorithm, including prohibiting robot automatic replies, etc.
Kentucky's encryption bill completes third reading
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Kentucky's crypto bill has completed the third reading, and is one step closer to the governor's signature. Earlier news, Kentucky Congressman TJ Roberts initiated the HB376 bill, proposing to invest 10% of state funds in digital assets (Bitcoin) with a market value of more than $750 billion. Kentucky is also the 16th state to propose a Bitcoin reserve bill. (Cointelegraph) Note: The third reading is a legislative procedure of the legislature. Because the title of the draft bill or motion will be read three times during this procedure, the procedure is called the third reading.

data.title: Kentucky's Cryptocurrency Bill Advances Closer to Governor's Approval
data.description: According to Odaily, a cryptocurrency bill in Kentucky has completed its third reading, moving it closer to being signed by the governor. The legislative process known as the third reading involves the title of the bill being read three times. Previously, Kentucky State Representative TJ Roberts introduced Bill HB376, which proposes investing 10% of the state's funds in digital assets with a market capitalization exceeding $750 billion, such as Bitcoin. Kentucky is the 16th state to propose a Bitcoin reserve bill.
